original_image_tags: A yellowed newspaper clipping from the "Washington Star" mounted on a larger, light-colored backing sheet. The clipping features two black-and-white photographs with captions, showing the wreckage of an aircraft. A date stamp and a handwritten number are visible at the bottom of the clipping.

A black-and-white photograph showing two men in uniform examining the large, crumpled wreckage of an aircraft. The aircraft appears to have a large, circular, fabric-covered rotor structure and a tail section with the visible marking "NX 91Y". The wreckage is in an outdoor, wooded area.

A black-and-white photograph showing two men in uniform examining a large, circular, wooden or metal ring-like structure. The structure has some internal framing and appears to be the "flying cheesebox" mentioned in the caption.

elements.description: Top photograph showing two uniformed men standing amidst the wreckage of a downed helicopter. A caption below the photograph reads: 'Troopers J. J. Harbaugh and Peter Kosirowsky of the Maryland State police are shown yesterday looking over remnants of Mr. Caldwell's helicopter, which had a pancakelike structure around the inner part of the rotors.'

Bottom photograph showing two uniformed men examining large circular metal parts and rotor blades inside a wooden structure. A caption below the photograph reads: 'State troopers with the "flying cheesebox" invented by Mr. Caldwell and found with his old helicopter in a tobacco shed on a farm near Glen Burnie, Md., after a search requested by the United States Air Force.'

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on two photographs showing uniformed men examining aircraft wreckage and a circular structure, but they provide different quoted caption text for the same visual elements.
